body:
## Summary

- Treat crates.io HTTP 429 responses as a deferred `publish_result=rate_limited` outcome instead of a hard release-script failure.
- Gate crate availability waiting, Docker Hub publishing, and GitHub release creation on either an already-published crate or `publish_result=success`.
- Add regression coverage for the deferred rate-limit path and update the issue #84 case study with follow-up run 25961456440.
- Added an upstream template follow-up: https://github.com/link-foundation/rust-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template/issues/57#issuecomment-4466926977

## Reproduction

Run 25961456440 showed the incomplete first fix: the publish step emitted `Output: publish_result=rate_limited`, then still ended with `Process completed with exit code 1.` The new script path exits successfully only for that deferred state, and the workflow skips downstream artifacts until a crate publish succeeds.

## Verification

- `rust-script --test scripts/publish-crate.rs`
- `cargo test --test unit ci_cd::workflow_release::release_workflow_defers_rate_limited_crates_publish_without_downstream_artifacts`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features`
- `cargo test --all-features --verbose`
- `cargo test --doc --verbose`
- `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs` (warnings only for existing near-limit files)
- `rust-script scripts/check-changelog-fragment.rs`
- `npm ci`
- `scripts/sync-seed.sh`
- `npx playwright test --config=playwright.local.config.js` after `npx playwright install chromium` (66 passed)



Fixes #84

commits.messageBody:
Adding .gitkeep for PR creation (default mode).
This file will be removed when the task is complete.

Issue: https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/84

title: fix(release): defer crates.io rate-limit retries
